data.text: I’ve confirmed the overlap: the CLI already has three separate status surfaces and the worker liveness TTL logic is centralized, but the bead-status text is still counting only persisted lifecycle state. Next I’m reading the exact command and resolver code plus the existing tests so I can wire them to one shared snapshot instead of patching them independently.

data.output:
1	package cmd
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"context"
     5		"encoding/json"
     6		"fmt"
     7		"strings"
     8		"time"
     9	
    10		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
    11		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
    12		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/workerstatus"
    13		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
    14	)
    15	
    16	// WorkFocusActiveWorker is one entry in the active_workers section.
    17	type WorkFocusActiveWorker struct {
    18		WorkerID       string `json:"worker_id"`
    19		CurrentBead    string `json:"current_bead,omitempty"`
    20		AttemptID      string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
    21		Phase          string `json:"phase,omitempty"`
    22		LastActivityAt string `json:"last_activity_at,omitempty"`
    23	}
    24	
    25	// WorkFocusBead is one item in the operator-attention section.
    26	type WorkFocusBead struct {
    27		ID              string `json:"id"`
    28		Title           string `json:"title"`
    29		Reason          string `json:"reason,omitempty"`
    30		SuggestedAction string `json:"suggested_action,omitempty"`
    31	}
    32	
    33	// WorkFocusBlockedBead is one item in the blocked_or_planning section.
    34	type WorkFocusBlockedBead struct {
    35		ID          string `json:"id"`
    36		Title       string `json:"title"`
    37		BlockerKind string `json:"blocker_kind"`
    38		Detail      string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
    39	}
    40	
    41	// WorkFocusReadySummary is the ready-queue depth summary.
    42	type WorkFocusReadySummary struct {
    43		Count int    `json:"count"`
    44		Depth string `json:"depth"` // "empty", "shallow", "moderate", "deep"
    45	}
    46	
    47	// WorkFocusReport is the structured result of "ddx work focus".
    48	type WorkFocusReport struct {
    49		HumanRequired        []WorkFocusBead         `json:"human_required"`
    50		BlockedOrPlanning    []WorkFocusBlockedBead  `json:"blocked_or_planning"`
    51		ReadySummary         WorkFocusReadySummary   `json:"ready_summary"`
    52		ActiveWorkers        []WorkFocusActiveWorker `json:"active_workers,omitempty"`
    53		WorkerRecommendation string                  `json:"worker_recommendation"`
    54		Unknowns             []string                `json:"unknowns"`
    55	}
    56	
    57	// workerReadyDepthLabel returns a human-readable depth label for the ready count.
    58	func workerReadyDepthLabel(count int) string {
    59		switch {
    60		case count == 0:
    61			return "empty"
    62		case count <= 1:
    63			return "shallow"
    64		case count <= 3:
    65			return "moderate"
    66		default:
    67			return "deep"
    68		}
    69	}
    70	
    71	// buildWorkFocusReport queries the store and returns a WorkFocusReport.
    72	//
    73	// projectRoot, when non-empty, enables the active_workers section: any
    74	// .ddx/workers/<id>/status.json sidecar with a last_activity_at within
    75	// workerstatus.LivenessTTL is reported as a live worker. This lets the
    76	// operator-facing view treat a long-running attempt as active even when the
    77	// bead tracker's claim timestamp has not changed (heartbeats are written
    78	// outside beads.jsonl to avoid tracker churn).
    79	func buildWorkFocusReport(store *bead.Store, projectRoot string) (WorkFocusReport, error) {
    80		// Collect operator-attention beads (status=proposed, any dep state).
    81		operatorAttentionBeads, err := store.ProposedOperatorAttention()
    82		if err != nil {
    83			return WorkFocusReport{}, fmt.Errorf("work focus: operator attention query: %w", err)
    84		}
    85	
    86		// Build a set of operator-attention IDs so we can exclude them from blocked section.
    87		operatorAttentionSet := make(map[string]bool, len(operatorAttentionBeads))
    88		humanRequired := make([]WorkFocusBead, 0, len(operatorAttentionBeads))
    89		for _, b := range operatorAttentionBeads {
    90			operatorAttentionSet[b.ID] = true
    91			meta := bead.GetNeedsHumanMeta(b)
    92			item := WorkFocusBead{
    93				ID:    b.ID,
    94				Title: b.Title,
    95			}
    96			if meta.Reason != "" {
    97				item.Reason = meta.Reason
    98			} else if meta.Summary != "" {
    99				item.Reason = meta.Summary
   100			}
   101			if meta.SuggestedAction != "" {
   102				item.SuggestedAction = meta.SuggestedAction
   103			}
   104			humanRequired = append(humanRequired, item)
   105		}
   106	
   107		// Collect all blocked beads; exclude operator-attention beads already captured above.
   108		allBlocked, err := store.BlockedAll()
   109		if err != nil {
   110			return WorkFocusReport{}, fmt.Errorf("work focus: blocked query: %w", err)
   111		}
   112		var blockedOrPlanning []WorkFocusBlockedBead
   113		for _, bb := range allBlocked {
   114			if operatorAttentionSet[bb.ID] {
   115				continue
   116			}
   117			item := WorkFocusBlockedBead{
   118				ID:          bb.ID,
   119				Title:       bb.Title,
   120				BlockerKind: bb.Blocker.Kind,
   121			}
   122			switch bb.Blocker.Kind {
   123			case bead.BlockerKindDependency:
   124				if len(bb.Blocker.UnclosedDepIDs) > 0 {
   125					item.Detail = "unclosed deps: " + strings.Join(bb.Blocker.UnclosedDepIDs, ", ")
   126				}
   127			case bead.BlockerKindRetryCooldown:
   128				if bb.Blocker.NextEligibleAt != "" {
   129					item.Detail = "retry after: " + bb.Blocker.NextEligibleAt
   130				}
   131			default:
   132				if bb.Blocker.Reason != "" {
   133					item.Detail = bb.Blocker.Reason
   134				}
   135			}
   136			blockedOrPlanning = append(blockedOrPlanning, item)
   137		}
   138	
   139		// Collect worker-ready depth.
   140		readyBeads, err := store.ReadyExecution()
   141		if err != nil {
   142			return WorkFocusReport{}, fmt.Errorf("work focus: ready query: %w", err)
   143		}
   144		readyCount := len(readyBeads)
   145		readySummary := WorkFocusReadySummary{
   146			Count: readyCount,
   147			Depth: workerReadyDepthLabel(readyCount),
   148		}
   149	
   150		// Observe in_progress beads as a capacity signal.
   151		inProgressBeads, err := store.List(bead.StatusInProgress, "", nil)
   152		if err != nil {
   153			return WorkFocusReport{}, fmt.Errorf("work focus: in_progress query: %w", err)
   154		}
   155		inProgressCount := len(inProgressBeads)
   156	
   157		// Active worker sidecars: workers whose status.json was updated within
   158		// workerstatus.LivenessTTL are treated as active even when the bead
   159		// tracker claim timestamp is older. This is the operator-facing answer
   160		// to "is the worker alive?" — the bead row alone cannot answer it
   161		// because heartbeats are recorded outside beads.jsonl.
   162		activeWorkers := collectActiveWorkers(projectRoot, time.Now())
   163	
   164		// Conservative worker recommendation. Treat each active sidecar as
   165		// observed capacity in addition to in_progress beads.
   166		workerRec := buildWorkerRecommendation(readyCount, inProgressCount+len(activeWorkers))
   167	
   168		// Unknowns: worker process liveness is not verifiable from the bead store.
   169		var unknowns []string
   170		if inProgressCount > 0 && len(activeWorkers) == 0 {
   171			unknowns = append(unknowns, fmt.Sprintf(
   172				"worker process liveness: %d bead(s) are in_progress but no active worker sidecar is fresh; check `ddx work status` for live processes",
   173				inProgressCount,
   174			))
   175		}
   176	
   177		return WorkFocusReport{
   178			HumanRequired:        humanRequired,
   179			BlockedOrPlanning:    blockedOrPlanning,
   180			ReadySummary:         readySummary,
   181			ActiveWorkers:        activeWorkers,
   182			WorkerRecommendation: workerRec,
   183			Unknowns:             unknowns,
   184		}, nil
   185	}
   186	
   187	// collectActiveWorkers reads .ddx/workers/*/status.json sidecars under
   188	// projectRoot and returns the ones whose last_activity_at is within
   189	// workerstatus.LivenessTTL. Empty projectRoot or a missing workers dir
   190	// yields nil.
   191	func collectActiveWorkers(projectRoot string, now time.Time) []WorkFocusActiveWorker {
   192		if projectRoot == "" {
   193			return nil
   194		}
   195		records, err := workerstatus.ListLiveness(projectRoot)
   196		if err != nil {
   197			return nil
   198		}
   199		var out []WorkFocusActiveWorker
   200		for _, rec := range records {
   201			if !rec.IsFresh(now) {
   202				continue
   203			}
   204			out = append(out, WorkFocusActiveWorker{
   205				WorkerID:       rec.WorkerID,
   206				CurrentBead:    rec.CurrentBead,
   207				AttemptID:      rec.AttemptID,
   208				Phase:          rec.Phase,
   209				LastActivityAt: rec.LastActivityAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
   210			})
   211		}
   212		return out
   213	}
   214	
   215	// buildWorkerRecommendation returns a conservative recommendation string.
   216	// It suggests another worker only when ready depth is high and capacity is observable.
   217	func buildWorkerRecommendation(readyCount, inProgressCount int) string {
   218		switch {
   219		case readyCount == 0:
   220			return "Queue is empty; no worker action needed."
   221		case inProgressCount > 0 && readyCount > 0:
   222			return fmt.Sprintf(
   223				"%d bead(s) ready; %d bead(s) currently claimed (in_progress). Monitor progress or run 'ddx work' to add capacity.",
   224				readyCount, inProgressCount,
   225			)
   226		case readyCount >= 3:
   227			return fmt.Sprintf(
   228				"%d bead(s) ready and no active workers detected; consider running: ddx work",
   229				readyCount,
   230			)
   231		default:
   232			return fmt.Sprintf(
   233				"%d bead(s) ready; run 'ddx work' to process.",
   234				readyCount,
   235			)
   236		}
   237	}
   238	
   239	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkFocusCommand() *cobra.Command {
   240		cmd := &cobra.Command{
   241			Use:   "focus",
   242			Short: "Show work requiring operator attention",
   243			Long: `focus shows a read-only summary of queue items that require operator
   244	attention — proposed beads, external blockers, dependency-waiting or
   245	planning-required beads — along with a conservative worker-capacity
   246	recommendation based on observable queue depth and in-progress signals.
   247	
   248	Unlike 'ddx work plan', focus is designed for interactive queue stewardship:
   249	it surfaces the items workers will NOT pick up and recommends operator actions.
   250	Worker-ready beads are not listed as primary intervention items; they appear
   251	only as a depth summary.
   252	
   253	Use --json for machine-readable output with stable keys:
   254	  human_required, blocked_or_planning, ready_summary,
   255	  worker_recommendation, unknowns
   256	`,
   257			Example: `  # Show intervention queue in human-readable format
   258	  ddx work focus
   259	
   260	  # Machine-readable output
   261	  ddx work focus --json | jq '.human_required'`,
   262			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
   263			RunE: f.runWorkFocus,
   264		}
   265		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output as JSON object with stable keys")
   266		return cmd
   267	}
   268	
   269	func (f *CommandFactory) runWorkFocus(cmd *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
   270		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
   271	
   272		ddxDir := ddxroot.Path(context.Background(), f.WorkingDir)
   273		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
   274	
   275		report, err := buildWorkFocusReport(store, f.WorkingDir)
   276		if err != nil {
   277			return err
   278		}
   279	
   280		if asJSON {
   281			enc := json.NewEncoder(cmd.OutOrStdout())
   282			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
   283			return enc.Encode(report)
   284		}
   285		return printWorkFocusText(cmd, report)
   286	}
   287	
   288	func printWorkFocusText(cmd *cobra.Command, r WorkFocusReport) error {
   289		out := cmd.OutOrStdout()
   290	
   291		// Section: Operator attention
   292		fmt.Fprintf(out, "=== Operator attention (%d) ===\n", len(r.HumanRequired))
   293		if len(r.HumanRequired) == 0 {
   294			fmt.Fprintln(out, "  (none)")
   295		} else {
   296			for _, item := range r.HumanRequired {
   297				fmt.Fprintf(out, "  [%s] %s\n", item.ID, item.Title)
   298				if item.Reason != "" {
   299					fmt.Fprintf(out, "       reason: %s\n", item.Reason)
   300				}
   301				if item.SuggestedAction != "" {
   302					fmt.Fprintf(out, "       suggested action: %s\n", item.SuggestedAction)
   303				}
   304			}
   305		}
   306	
   307		// Section: Blocked / planning
   308		fmt.Fprintf(out, "\n=== Blocked / planning (%d) ===\n", len(r.BlockedOrPlanning))
   309		if len(r.BlockedOrPlanning) == 0 {
   310			fmt.Fprintln(out, "  (none)")
   311		} else {
   312			for _, item := range r.BlockedOrPlanning {
   313				fmt.Fprintf(out, "  [%s] %s\n", item.ID, item.Title)
   314				if item.Detail != "" {
   315					fmt.Fprintf(out, "       blocker: %s — %s\n", item.BlockerKind, item.Detail)
   316				} else {
   317					fmt.Fprintf(out, "       blocker: %s\n", item.BlockerKind)
   318				}
   319			}
   320		}

data.text: I’ve got the core seam now: `bead status` is raw lifecycle counts, `work focus` already has its own sidecar-based active worker view, and GraphQL `QueueSummary` independently folds in run-state. I’m reading the surrounding tests to see which existing helpers can be reused and whether the fix should live in `cmd`, `internal/bead`, or a small shared package.

    20	type fixedScanner struct {
    21		workers []workerstatus.LiveWorker
    22	}
    23	
    24	func (s fixedScanner) Scan(_ context.Context) ([]workerstatus.LiveWorker, error) {
    25		return s.workers, nil
    26	}
    27	
    28	// TestWorkStatusDefaultsToCurrentProjectOnly proves the default scope of
    29	// `ddx work status --json` is the requested project root, not every live
    30	// ddx worker on the host. This is the regression test for the originating
    31	// incident: a global `ps | grep ddx work` scan answered "is project A's
    32	// worker running?" with a worker that actually belonged to project B.
    33	func TestWorkStatusDefaultsToCurrentProjectOnly(t *testing.T) {
    34		projectA := t.TempDir()
    35		projectB := t.TempDir()
    36	
    37		scannerWorkers := []workerstatus.LiveWorker{
    38			{
    39				PID:         101,
    40				Command:     "ddx work --once --project " + projectA,
    41				ProjectRoot: projectA,
    42				StartedAt:   time.Now().Add(-10 * time.Minute).UTC(),
    43				Age:         "10m",
    44				AgeSeconds:  600,
    45			},
    46			{
    47				PID:         202,
    48				Command:     "ddx work --once --project " + projectB,
    49				ProjectRoot: projectB,
    50				StartedAt:   time.Now().Add(-3 * time.Minute).UTC(),
    51				Age:         "3m",
    52				AgeSeconds:  180,
    53			},
    54		}
    55	
    56		factory := NewCommandFactory(projectA)
    57		factory.workerScannerOverride = fixedScanner{workers: scannerWorkers}
    58		root := factory.NewRootCommand()
    59	
    60		out, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "status", "--project", projectA, "--json")
    61		require.NoError(t, err)
    62	
    63		var report WorkStatusReport
    64		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &report))
    65	
    66		assert.Equal(t, "project", report.Scope)
    67		assert.Equal(t, projectA, report.ProjectRoot)
    68		require.Len(t, report.Workers, 1, "default scope must filter to requested project only; got %s", out)
    69		assert.Equal(t, 101, report.Workers[0].PID)
    70		assert.Equal(t, projectA, report.Workers[0].ProjectRoot)
    71	}

    73	// TestWorkStatusAllProjectsShowsExplicitGlobalView proves --all-projects
    74	// returns every live worker (labelled with their own project_root) so an

   273		assert.Equal(t, worktree, report.Workers[0].ExecutionWorktree)

   276	func TestWorkStatusUsesFreshLivenessSidecarForActiveAttempt(t *testing.T) {
   277		if runtime.GOOS != "linux" {
   278			t.Skip("process cwd inspection for sidecar child pid is linux-only")
   279		}
   280	
   281		projectRoot := t.TempDir()
   282		worktree := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), ".execute-bead-wt-ddx-aabbccdd-20260515T120840-2c0aa694")
   283		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(worktree, 0o755))
   284	
   285		child := exec.Command("sleep", "30")
   286		child.Dir = worktree
   287		require.NoError(t, child.Start())
   288		t.Cleanup(func() {
   289			if child.Process != nil {
   290				_ = child.Process.Kill()
   291				_ = child.Wait()
   292			}
   293		})
   294	
   295		startedAt := time.Now().Add(-2 * time.Minute).UTC()
   296		parentPID := 4242
   297		scannerWorkers := []workerstatus.LiveWorker{
   298			{
   299				PID:         parentPID,
   300				Command:     "ddx work --watch --project " + projectRoot,
   301				ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
   302				StartedAt:   startedAt,
   303				Age:         "2m",
   304				AgeSeconds:  120,
   305			},
   306		}
   307		require.NoError(t, workerstatus.WriteLiveness(projectRoot, "worker-active", workerstatus.LivenessRecord{
   308			WorkerID:       "worker-active",
   309			ProjectRoot:    projectRoot,
   310			CurrentBead:    "ddx-aabbccdd",
   311			AttemptID:      "20260515T120840-2c0aa694",
   312			Phase:          "running",
   313			PID:            parentPID,
   314			ChildPID:       child.Process.Pid,
   315			StartedAt:      startedAt,
   316			LastActivityAt: time.Now().UTC(),
   317		}))
   318	
   319		factory := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot)
   320		factory.workerScannerOverride = fixedScanner{workers: scannerWorkers}
   321		root := factory.NewRootCommand()
   322	
   323		textOut, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "status", "--project", projectRoot)
   324		require.NoError(t, err)
   325	
   326		assert.Contains(t, textOut, "bead=ddx-aabbccdd")
   327		assert.Contains(t, textOut, "attempt=20260515T120840-2c0aa694")
   328		assert.Contains(t, textOut, "worktree="+worktree)
   329		assert.NotContains(t, textOut, "bead=-")
   330	
   331		jsonOut, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "work", "status", "--project", projectRoot, "--json")
   332		require.NoError(t, err)
   333	
   334		var report WorkStatusReport
   335		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(jsonOut), &report))
   336		require.Len(t, report.Workers, 1)
   337		assert.Equal(t, "ddx-aabbccdd", report.Workers[0].BeadID)
   338		assert.Equal(t, "20260515T120840-2c0aa694", report.Workers[0].AttemptID)
   339		assert.Equal(t, "running", report.Workers[0].Phase)
   340		assert.Equal(t, child.Process.Pid, report.Workers[0].ChildPID)
   341		assert.Equal(t, worktree, report.Workers[0].ExecutionWorktree)
   342		assert.False(t, report.Workers[0].LastActivityAt.IsZero())
   343	}

   433			Phase:          "running",

   438	
   439		factory := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot)
   440		factory.workerScannerOverride = fixedScanner{workers: scannerWorkers}
   441		root := factory.NewRootCommand()

    18	// setupWorkFocusEnv initializes a test environment and populates its bead store.
    19	func setupWorkFocusEnv(t *testing.T, beads ...*bead.Bead) *TestEnvironment {
    20		t.Helper()
    21		env := NewTestEnvironment(t)
    22		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(env.Dir, ddxroot.DirName))
    23		require.NoError(t, store.Init(context.Background()))
    24		for _, b := range beads {
    25			require.NoError(t, store.Create(context.Background(), b))
    26		}
    27		return env
    28	}

    30	// TestWorkFocusReportsInterventionQueue verifies that proposed/operator-attention
    31	// beads appear in human_required, and dependency-waiting beads appear
    32	// in blocked_or_planning.
    33	func TestWorkFocusReportsInterventionQueue(t *testing.T) {
    34		blocker := &bead.Bead{
    35			ID:    "ddx-focus-blocker",
    36			Title: "Blocker bead",
    37		}
    38		// Proposed bead (operator attention required).
    39		nhBead := &bead.Bead{
    40			ID:     "ddx-focus-nh",
    41			Title:  "Needs human attention",
    42			Status: bead.StatusProposed,
    43			Labels: []string{bead.LabelNeedsHuman},
    44			Extra: map[string]any{
    45				bead.ExtraNeedsHumanReason: "review returned terminal BLOCK",
    46			},
    47		}
    48		// Dependency-waiting bead (planning required before work can proceed).
    49		depBlocked := &bead.Bead{
    50			ID:    "ddx-focus-dep",
    51			Title: "Dep-blocked planning bead",
    52		}
    53		depBlocked.AddDep(blocker.ID, "blocks")
    54	
    55		env := setupWorkFocusEnv(t, blocker, nhBead, depBlocked)
    56		root := NewCommandFactory(env.Dir).NewRootCommand()
    57	
    58		out, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "focus", "--json")
    59		require.NoError(t, err)
    60	
    61		var report WorkFocusReport
    62		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &report))
    63	
    64		// Proposed bead must appear in human_required.
    65		var foundNH bool
    66		for _, item := range report.HumanRequired {
    67			if item.ID == nhBead.ID {
    68				foundNH = true
    69				assert.Equal(t, "review returned terminal BLOCK", item.Reason)
    70			}
    71		}
    72		assert.True(t, foundNH, "proposed bead must appear in human_required")
    73	
    74		// Proposed bead must NOT appear in blocked_or_planning.
    75		for _, item := range report.BlockedOrPlanning {
    76			assert.NotEqual(t, nhBead.ID, item.ID, "proposed bead must not appear in blocked_or_planning")
    77		}
    78	
    79		// Dependency-waiting bead must appear in blocked_or_planning.
    80		var foundDep bool
    81		for _, item := range report.BlockedOrPlanning {
    82			if item.ID == depBlocked.ID {
    83				foundDep = true
    84				assert.Equal(t, bead.BlockerKindDependency, item.BlockerKind)
    85			}
    86		}
    87		assert.True(t, foundDep, "dependency-waiting bead must appear in blocked_or_planning")
    88	}

    90	// TestWorkFocusOmitsWorkerReadyBeadsByDefault verifies that worker-ready
    91	// (execution-eligible) beads are NOT listed as primary intervention items.
    92	// They must appear only as a depth count in ready_summary.
    93	func TestWorkFocusOmitsWorkerReadyBeadsByDefault(t *testing.T) {
    94		readyBead := &bead.Bead{
    95			ID:    "ddx-focus-ready",
    96			Title: "Ready for worker",
    97		}
    98		nhBead := &bead.Bead{
    99			ID:     "ddx-focus-nh2",
   100			Title:  "Needs human",
   101			Status: bead.StatusProposed,
   102			Labels: []string{bead.LabelNeedsHuman},
   103		}
   104	
   105		env := setupWorkFocusEnv(t, readyBead, nhBead)
   106		root := NewCommandFactory(env.Dir).NewRootCommand()
   107	
   108		out, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "focus", "--json")
   109		require.NoError(t, err)
   110	
   111		var report WorkFocusReport
   112		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &report))
   113	
   114		// Worker-ready bead must NOT appear in human_required.
   115		for _, item := range report.HumanRequired {
   116			assert.NotEqual(t, readyBead.ID, item.ID, "worker-ready bead must not be in human_required")
   117		}
   118		// Worker-ready bead must NOT appear in blocked_or_planning.
   119		for _, item := range report.BlockedOrPlanning {
   120			assert.NotEqual(t, readyBead.ID, item.ID, "worker-ready bead must not be in blocked_or_planning")
   121		}
   122		// The ready_summary must reflect the ready bead.
   123		assert.Equal(t, 1, report.ReadySummary.Count)
   124		assert.NotEmpty(t, report.ReadySummary.Depth)
   125	
   126		// The proposed bead must appear in human_required.
   127		var foundNH bool
   128		for _, item := range report.HumanRequired {
   129			if item.ID == nhBead.ID {
   130				foundNH = true
   131			}
   132		}
   133		assert.True(t, foundNH, "proposed bead must still appear in human_required")
   134	}

   136	// TestWorkFocusJSONIncludesUnknownHazards verifies that the JSON output's
   137	// unknowns field surfaces capacity signals when in_progress beads are present.
   138	func TestWorkFocusJSONIncludesUnknownHazards(t *testing.T) {

   211	// TestWorkFocusReportsActiveLongRunningWorkerFromWorkerStatus verifies AC #5:
   212	// a fresh worker-status sidecar (last_activity_at within LivenessTTL) is
   213	// reported as an active worker by `ddx work focus`, even when the bead
   214	// tracker's claim timestamp has not moved. Without the sidecar, the same
   215	// bead would appear merely as in_progress + an "unknown liveness" hazard.
   216	func TestWorkFocusReportsActiveLongRunningWorkerFromWorkerStatus(t *testing.T) {
   217		b := &bead.Bead{
   218			ID:    "ddx-focus-active-worker",
   219			Title: "In-progress bead with live worker",
   220		}
   221		env := setupWorkFocusEnv(t, b)
   222	
   223		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(env.Dir, ddxroot.DirName))
   224		require.NoError(t, store.Claim(b.ID, "test-worker"))
   225	
   226		// Write a fresh worker status sidecar so the focus report can detect
   227		// the live worker without consulting the (stale-by-design) tracker
   228		// claim timestamp.
   229		workerID := "wkr-focus-active"
   230		require.NoError(t, workerstatus.WriteLiveness(env.Dir, workerID, workerstatus.LivenessRecord{
   231			WorkerID:       workerID,
   232			ProjectRoot:    env.Dir,
   233			CurrentBead:    b.ID,
   234			AttemptID:      "att-focus-001",
   235			Phase:          "running",
   236			Harness:        "claude",
   237			Model:          "opus",
   238			PID:            4242,
   239			LastActivityAt: time.Now().UTC(),
   240		}))
   241	
   242		root := NewCommandFactory(env.Dir).NewRootCommand()
   243		out, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "focus", "--json")
   244		require.NoError(t, err)
   245	
   246		var report WorkFocusReport
   247		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &report))
   248	
   249		require.Len(t, report.ActiveWorkers, 1,
   250			"focus must surface the live worker sidecar even when the tracker claim timestamp has not changed")
   251		assert.Equal(t, workerID, report.ActiveWorkers[0].WorkerID)
   252		assert.Equal(t, b.ID, report.ActiveWorkers[0].CurrentBead)
   253		assert.Equal(t, "att-focus-001", report.ActiveWorkers[0].AttemptID)
   254		assert.Equal(t, "running", report.ActiveWorkers[0].Phase)
   255	
   256		// The in_progress liveness "unknown" hazard must NOT be added when an
   257		// active sidecar is present — otherwise the focus report would
   258		// contradict itself.
   259		for _, u := range report.Unknowns {
   260			assert.NotContains(t, u, "worker process liveness",
   261				"active sidecar must suppress the in_progress liveness unknown")
   262		}
   263	}
   264	
   265	// TestWorkFocusJSONStableKeys verifies AC3: --json returns the five stable keys.
   266	func TestWorkFocusJSONStableKeys(t *testing.T) {
   267		env := setupWorkFocusEnv(t)
   268		root := NewCommandFactory(env.Dir).NewRootCommand()
   269	
   270		out, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "focus", "--json")
   271		require.NoError(t, err)
   272	
   273		var raw map[string]json.RawMessage
   274		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &raw))
   275	
   276		for _, key := range []string{"human_required", "blocked_or_planning", "ready_summary", "worker_recommendation", "unknowns"} {
   277			assert.Contains(t, raw, key, "JSON output must contain stable key: %s", key)
   278		}
   279	}

2570	  """
  2571	  workerProgress(
  2572	    """Worker identifier"""
  2573	    workerID: ID!
  2574	  ): [PhaseTransition!]!
  2575	
  2576	  """Return captured stdout/stderr from a worker (maps to GET /api/agent/workers/{id}/log)"""
  2577	  workerLog(
  2578	    """Worker identifier"""
  2579	    workerID: ID!

  2581

  2583	  Return the prompt text for a worker's current or last attempt (maps to GET /api/agent/workers/{id}/prompt)
  2584	  """
  2585	  workerPrompt(
  2586	    """Worker identifier"""
  2587	    workerID: ID!
  2588	  ): String!
  2589	
  2590	  """
  2591	  List workers from two sources: the worker_ingest derived view (ADR-022 §Worker-server interface)
  2592	  and in-flight attempts from live run-state.json (for inline work). Provides unified visibility
  2593	  into both registered worker reports and live attempts without polling the bead tracker.
  2594	  Worker panes (dashboard, workers listing) primarily use this query.
  2595	  Distinct from the `workers` query, which surfaces the on-disk worker registry.
  2596	  Each entry is classified connected/stale/disconnected by elapsed time since last_event_at
  2597	  per the rev-5 freshness state model.

data.text: I found the likely collision point: some commands are treating “active” as a process-sidecar concept, others as tracker lifecycle, and GraphQL still has its own count aggregation. I’m now checking the claim/liveness helpers to see whether the fix belongs in the bead store itself or in a new reconciliation helper that can be reused by all three surfaces.

cli/cmd/shared_helpers.go:20:func resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, workingDir string) string {
     1	package cmd
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"encoding/json"
     5		"fmt"
     6		"io"
     7		"strings"
     8		"time"
     9	
    10		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
    11		workguard "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent/work"
    12		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/escalation"
    13		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/lockmetrics"
    14		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
    15	)
    16	
    17	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
    18	// queue drain. ddx work treats --harness,
    19	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
    20	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
    21	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
    22	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
    23		cmd := &cobra.Command{
    24			Use:   "work",
    25			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
    26			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
    27	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
    28	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
    29	
    30	ddx work treats --harness, --provider, --model, and --profile as opaque
    31	passthrough constraints forwarded to Fizeau unchanged. DDx does not validate
    32	these values or branch on them; Fizeau owns routing within the requested power
    33	bounds.
    34	
    35	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
    36	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
    37	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
    38	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
    39	
    40	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
    41	  drained     — no ready beads remain
    42	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
    43	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
    44	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
    45	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
    46	
    47	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
    48	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
    49	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
    50	`,
    51			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
    52	  ddx work
    53	
    54	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
    55	  ddx work --once
    56	
    57	  # Watch for newly-ready work after the current queue drains
    58	  ddx work --watch
    59	
    60	  # Watch with a shorter idle scan interval
    61	  ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s
    62	
    63	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
    64	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
    65	
    66	  # Skip review only with the break-glass acknowledgement flag
    67	  ddx work --once --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing
    68	
    69	  # Constrain power powerClass (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
    70	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
    71			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
    72			RunE: f.runWork,
    73		}
    74	
    75		// Routing knobs — passthrough only. ddx work forwards these to the agent
    76		// unchanged and does not validate or branch on their string values.
    77		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
    78		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
    79		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
    80		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
    81		cmd.Flags().String("profile", "", "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart (empty = unconstrained; let the agent service choose)")
    82		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
    83		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
    84		cmd.Flags().String("attempt-backend", "", "Attempt backend: worktree, local-clone, docker-clone, or in-tree (default: executions.attempt_backend)")
    85		cmd.Flags().Bool("ignore-cooldown", false, "Ignore retry cooldown across this worker session (requires --reason)")
    86		cmd.Flags().String("reason", "", "Operator reason required by --ignore-cooldown")
    87		cmd.Flags().Bool("once", false, "Process at most one ready bead")
    88		cmd.Flags().Bool("watch", false, "Keep watching for newly-ready beads after the current queue drains")
    89		cmd.Flags().Bool("self-refresh", false, "Force re-exec onto a newer installed ddx binary between iterations (on by default in watch mode; use --no-self-refresh to disable)")
    90		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-self-refresh", false, "Disable self-refresh: do NOT re-exec onto a newer installed ddx binary between watch iterations (self-refresh is on by default in watch mode)")
    91		cmd.Flags().Duration("idle-interval", 30*time.Second, "Sleep duration between empty-queue scans in watch mode")
    92		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")
    93		cmd.Flags().Bool("local", false, "Deprecated: no-op; ddx work always runs inline (ADR-022)")
    94		_ = cmd.Flags().MarkDeprecated("local", "ddx work always runs inline; the flag is a no-op (ADR-022)")
    95		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review (break-glass: requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing)")
    96		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing", false, "Break-glass acknowledgement required when using --no-review")
    97		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
    98		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart powerClass)")
    99		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD, "Stop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited")
   100		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-bead-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxBeadCostUSD, "Per-bead cost budget in USD; stop escalating when this bead's billed cost exceeds this amount (0 = unlimited); overridden per-bead by a budget:<USD> label")
   101		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-recovery-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxRecoveryCostUSD, "Per-bead automated recovery budget in USD for reframe/decompose attempts after repeated ladder exhaustion")
   102		cmd.Flags().Int("claim-rate-window", agent.DefaultClaimSuccessRateWindow, "Rolling window of recent claim attempts used to compute the claim success rate warning")
   103		cmd.Flags().Float64("claim-rate-threshold", agent.DefaultClaimSuccessRateThreshold, "Warn when the rolling claim success rate over a full window is at or below this threshold")
   104		cmd.Flags().Int("preclaim-warn-threshold", agent.DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold, "Consecutive identical pre-claim warn fingerprints across distinct bead IDs required before the loop emits operator attention")
   105		cmd.Flags().Duration("preclaim-timeout", workguard.DefaultPreClaimTimeout, "Pre-claim readiness timeout for preflight/readiness hooks")
   106		cmd.Flags().Duration("route-resolution-timeout", agent.DefaultRouteResolutionTimeout, "Timeout bounding routing preflight and the resolveRoute viability check; on expiry the lease is released and the bead is flagged for operator attention")
   107		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
   108		// Per-bead rate-limit retry budget (ddx-c6e3db02 / TD-031 §8.4).
   109		cmd.Flags().Duration("rate-limit-max-wait", agent.RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget,
   110			"Per-bead total wait budget for HTTP 429 / rate-limit retries (default 5m). 0 keeps the default; negative disables retry.")
   111		// Power bounds: ddx work owns retry-power policy — these set the envelope
   112		// for operator-constrained power within which the agent selects the route.
   113		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
   114		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
   115	
   116		// Register "ddx work plan", "ddx work focus", "ddx work clear-cooldowns",
   117		// "ddx work metrics", "ddx work analyze", and "ddx work status" as
   118		// subcommands.
   119		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkPlanCommand())
   120		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkFocusCommand())
   121		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkClearCooldownsCommand())
   122		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkMetricsCommand())
   123		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkAnalyzeCommand())
   124		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkStatusCommand())
   125	
   126		return cmd
   127	}
   128	
   129	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
   130	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
   131	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
   132	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
   133	// by this layer.
   134	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
   135		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
   136			return err
   137		}
   138	
   139		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
   140		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
   141		lockmetrics.SetSink(lockmetrics.FileSink(projectRoot))
   142		lockmetrics.SetCapEnforcement(projectRoot, "")
   143		f.warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource(cmd)
   144	
   145		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
   146		// Suppressed in JSON mode to avoid corrupting machine-readable output
   147		// (stderr and stdout are merged by some test helpers and log aggregators).
   148		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
   149		if !asJSON {
   150			preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
   151			f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
   152				emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
   153			})
   154		}
   155	
   156		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true, "")
   157	}
   158	
   159	func writeExecuteLoopResult(w io.Writer, projectRoot string, result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult, asJSON bool) error {
   160		if asJSON {
   161			payload := struct {
   162				ProjectRoot string `json:"project_root"`
   163				*agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult
   164			}{
   165				ProjectRoot:           projectRoot,
   166				ExecuteBeadLoopResult: result,
   167			}
   168			enc := json.NewEncoder(w)
   169			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
   170			return enc.Encode(payload)
   171		}
   172	
   173		if result.NoReadyWork && result.Attempts == 0 {
   174			writeNoReadyWorkSummary(w, projectRoot, result.NoReadyWorkDetail)
   175			writeQueueSnapshotTerminalSummary(w, result.QueueSnapshot)
   176			return nil
   177		}
   178	
   179		fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nproject: %s\n", projectRoot)
   180		writeWorkTerminalSummary(w, result)
   181		writeOperatorAttentionSummary(w, result.OperatorAttention)
   182		if result.Failures > 0 {
   183			fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nfailed:\n")
   184			for _, attempt := range result.Results {
   185				if attempt.Status != "success" {
   186					fmt.Fprintf(w, "  - %s: %s", attempt.BeadID, attempt.Detail)
   187					if attempt.PreserveRef != "" {
   188						fmt.Fprintf(w, " (preserved)")
   189					}
   190					fmt.Fprintln(w)
   191				}
   192			}
   193		}
   194		if result.NoReadyWork {
   195			fmt.Fprintln(w)
   196			writeNoReadyWorkSummary(w, "", result.NoReadyWorkDetail)
   197			writeQueueSnapshotTerminalSummary(w, result.QueueSnapshot)
   198		}
   199		return nil
   200	}
   201	
   202	func writeWorkTerminalSummary(w io.Writer, result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) {
   203		closed, changed, alreadySatisfied := countWorkTerminalOutcomes(result)
   204		fmt.Fprintf(w, "worker exited: %s\n", workExitSummary(result))
   205		fmt.Fprintf(w, "attempts: %d  |  closed: %d  |  changed: %d  |  already-satisfied: %d  |  failures: %d\n",
   206			result.Attempts, closed, changed, alreadySatisfied, result.Failures)
   207	}
   208	
   209	func writeOperatorAttentionSummary(w io.Writer, stop *agent.OperatorAttentionStop) {
   210		if stop == nil {
   211			return
   212		}
   213		fmt.Fprintln(w)
   214		fmt.Fprintln(w, "operator attention:")
   215		if stop.BeadID != "" {
   216			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  released bead: %s\n", stop.BeadID)
   217		}
   218		if stop.Message != "" {
   219			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  %s\n", stop.Message)
   220		}

190	// Default values
   191	const (
   192		DefaultType          = "task"
   193		DefaultStatus        = StatusOpen
   194		DefaultPriority      = 2
   195		DefaultPrefix        = "bx" // used only when repo name detection fails
   196		CurrentSchemaVersion = 1
   197		MinPriority          = 0
   198		MaxPriority          = 4
   199	)
   200	
   201	// StatusCounts holds aggregate counts for a bead store.
   202	type StatusCounts struct {
   203		Open              int `json:"open"`
   204		InProgress        int `json:"in_progress"`
   205		Closed            int `json:"closed"`
   206		Blocked           int `json:"blocked"`
   207		Proposed          int `json:"proposed"`
   208		Cancelled         int `json:"cancelled"`
   209		Ready             int `json:"ready"`
   210		NeedsHuman        int `json:"needs_human"`
   211		WorkerReady       int `json:"worker_ready"`
   212		DependencyWaiting int `json:"dependency_waiting"`
   213		ExternalBlocked   int `json:"external_blocked"`
   214		OperatorAttention int `json:"operator_attention"`
   215		Total             int `json:"total"`
   216	}
   217	
   218	// Blocker kinds surfaced through BlockedAll. These strings are part of the
   219	// external DDx contract (HELIX reads them to decide how to handle a blocker).
   220	const (
   221		BlockerKindDependency         = "dependency"
   222		BlockerKindBlockedStatus      = "blocked-status"
   223		BlockerKindRetryCooldown      = "retry-cooldown"
   224		BlockerKindNeedsInvestigation = "needs-investigation"
   225		BlockerKindOperatorAttention  = "operator-attention"
   226		BlockerKindNotEligible        = "not-execution-eligible"
   227		BlockerKindSuperseded         = "superseded"
   228		BlockerKindEpicOnly           = "epic-only"
   229	)
   230	
   231	// Blocker describes why an open bead is currently not runnable. Either
   232	// unclosed dependencies exist, or an work cooldown has parked the
   233	// bead until NextEligibleAt.

    48	type WorkFocusReport struct {

    56	
    57	// workerReadyDepthLabel returns a human-readable depth label for the ready count.
    58	func workerReadyDepthLabel(count int) string {
    59		switch {
    60		case count == 0:
    61			return "empty"
    62		case count <= 1:
    63			return "shallow"
    64		case count <= 3:
    65			return "moderate"
    66		default:
    67			return "deep"
    68		}
    69	}
    70	
    71	// buildWorkFocusReport queries the store and returns a WorkFocusReport.
    72	//
    73	// projectRoot, when non-empty, enables the active_workers section: any
    74	// .ddx/workers/<id>/status.json sidecar with a last_activity_at within
    75	// workerstatus.LivenessTTL is reported as a live worker. This lets the
    76	// operator-facing view treat a long-running attempt as active even when the
    77	// bead tracker's claim timestamp has not changed (heartbeats are written
    78	// outside beads.jsonl to avoid tracker churn).
    79	func buildWorkFocusReport(store *bead.Store, projectRoot string) (WorkFocusReport, error) {
    80		// Collect operator-attention beads (status=proposed, any dep state).
    81		operatorAttentionBeads, err := store.ProposedOperatorAttention()
    82		if err != nil {
    83			return WorkFocusReport{}, fmt.Errorf("work focus: operator attention query: %w", err)
    84		}
    85	
    86		// Build a set of operator-attention IDs so we can exclude them from blocked section.
    87		operatorAttentionSet := make(map[string]bool, len(operatorAttentionBeads))
    88		humanRequired := make([]WorkFocusBead, 0, len(operatorAttentionBeads))
    89		for _, b := range operatorAttentionBeads {
    90			operatorAttentionSet[b.ID] = true
    91			meta := bead.GetNeedsHumanMeta(b)
    92			item := WorkFocusBead{
    93				ID:    b.ID,
    94				Title: b.Title,
    95			}
    96			if meta.Reason != "" {
    97				item.Reason = meta.Reason
    98			} else if meta.Summary != "" {
    99				item.Reason = meta.Summary
   100			}
   101			if meta.SuggestedAction != "" {
   102				item.SuggestedAction = meta.SuggestedAction
   103			}
   104			humanRequired = append(humanRequired, item)
   105		}
   106	
   107		// Collect all blocked beads; exclude operator-attention beads already captured above.
   108		allBlocked, err := store.BlockedAll()
   109		if err != nil {
   110			return WorkFocusReport{}, fmt.Errorf("work focus: blocked query: %w", err)
   111		}
   112		var blockedOrPlanning []WorkFocusBlockedBead
   113		for _, bb := range allBlocked {
   114			if operatorAttentionSet[bb.ID] {
   115				continue
   116			}
   117			item := WorkFocusBlockedBead{
   118				ID:          bb.ID,
   119				Title:       bb.Title,
   120				BlockerKind: bb.Blocker.Kind,
   121			}
   122			switch bb.Blocker.Kind {
   123			case bead.BlockerKindDependency:
   124				if len(bb.Blocker.UnclosedDepIDs) > 0 {
   125					item.Detail = "unclosed deps: " + strings.Join(bb.Blocker.UnclosedDepIDs, ", ")
   126				}
   127			case bead.BlockerKindRetryCooldown:
   128				if bb.Blocker.NextEligibleAt != "" {
   129					item.Detail = "retry after: " + bb.Blocker.NextEligibleAt
   130				}
   131			default:
   132				if bb.Blocker.Reason != "" {
   133					item.Detail = bb.Blocker.Reason
   134				}
   135			}
   136			blockedOrPlanning = append(blockedOrPlanning, item)
   137		}
   138	
   139		// Collect worker-ready depth.
   140		readyBeads, err := store.ReadyExecution()
   141		if err != nil {
   142			return WorkFocusReport{}, fmt.Errorf("work focus: ready query: %w", err)
   143		}
   144		readyCount := len(readyBeads)
   145		readySummary := WorkFocusReadySummary{
   146			Count: readyCount,
   147			Depth: workerReadyDepthLabel(readyCount),
   148		}
   149	
   150		// Observe in_progress beads as a capacity signal.
   151		inProgressBeads, err := store.List(bead.StatusInProgress, "", nil)
   152		if err != nil {
   153			return WorkFocusReport{}, fmt.Errorf("work focus: in_progress query: %w", err)
   154		}
   155		inProgressCount := len(inProgressBeads)
   156	
   157		// Active worker sidecars: workers whose status.json was updated within
   158		// workerstatus.LivenessTTL are treated as active even when the bead
   159		// tracker claim timestamp is older. This is the operator-facing answer
   160		// to "is the worker alive?" — the bead row alone cannot answer it
   161		// because heartbeats are recorded outside beads.jsonl.
   162		activeWorkers := collectActiveWorkers(projectRoot, time.Now())
   163	
   164		// Conservative worker recommendation. Treat each active sidecar as
   165		// observed capacity in addition to in_progress beads.
   166		workerRec := buildWorkerRecommendation(readyCount, inProgressCount+len(activeWorkers))
   167	
   168		// Unknowns: worker process liveness is not verifiable from the bead store.
   169		var unknowns []string
   170		if inProgressCount > 0 && len(activeWorkers) == 0 {
   171			unknowns = append(unknowns, fmt.Sprintf(
   172				"worker process liveness: %d bead(s) are in_progress but no active worker sidecar is fresh; check `ddx work status` for live processes",
   173				inProgressCount,
   174			))
   175		}
   176	
   177		return WorkFocusReport{
   178			HumanRequired:        humanRequired,
   179			BlockedOrPlanning:    blockedOrPlanning,
   180			ReadySummary:         readySummary,
   181			ActiveWorkers:        activeWorkers,
   182			WorkerRecommendation: workerRec,
   183			Unknowns:             unknowns,
   184		}, nil
   185	}
   186	
   187	// collectActiveWorkers reads .ddx/workers/*/status.json sidecars under
   188	// projectRoot and returns the ones whose last_activity_at is within
   189	// workerstatus.LivenessTTL. Empty projectRoot or a missing workers dir
   190	// yields nil.
   191	func collectActiveWorkers(projectRoot string, now time.Time) []WorkFocusActiveWorker {
   192		if projectRoot == "" {
   193			return nil
   194		}
   195		records, err := workerstatus.ListLiveness(projectRoot)
   196		if err != nil {
   197			return nil
   198		}
   199		var out []WorkFocusActiveWorker
   200		for _, rec := range records {
   201			if !rec.IsFresh(now) {
   202				continue
   203			}
   204			out = append(out, WorkFocusActiveWorker{
   205				WorkerID:       rec.WorkerID,
   206				CurrentBead:    rec.CurrentBead,
   207				AttemptID:      rec.AttemptID,
   208				Phase:          rec.Phase,
   209				LastActivityAt: rec.LastActivityAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
   210			})
   211		}
   212		return out
   213	}
   214	
   215	// buildWorkerRecommendation returns a conservative recommendation string.
   216	// It suggests another worker only when ready depth is high and capacity is observable.
   217	func buildWorkerRecommendation(readyCount, inProgressCount int) string {
   218		switch {
   219		case readyCount == 0:
   220			return "Queue is empty; no worker action needed."
   221		case inProgressCount > 0 && readyCount > 0:
   222			return fmt.Sprintf(
   223				"%d bead(s) ready; %d bead(s) currently claimed (in_progress). Monitor progress or run 'ddx work' to add capacity.",
   224				readyCount, inProgressCount,
   225			)
   226		case readyCount >= 3:
   227			return fmt.Sprintf(
   228				"%d bead(s) ready and no active workers detected; consider running: ddx work",
   229				readyCount,
   230			)
   231		default:
   232			return fmt.Sprintf(
   233				"%d bead(s) ready; run 'ddx work' to process.",
   234				readyCount,
   235			)
   236		}
   237	}

   212	// TestWorkFocusReportsActiveLongRunningWorkerFromWorkerStatus verifies AC #5:
   213	// a fresh worker-status sidecar (last_activity_at within LivenessTTL) is
   214	// reported as an active worker by `ddx work focus`, even when the bead
   215	// tracker's claim timestamp has not moved. Without the sidecar, the same
   216	// bead would appear merely as in_progress + an "unknown liveness" hazard.
   217	func TestWorkFocusReportsActiveLongRunningWorkerFromWorkerStatus(t *testing.T) {
   218		b := &bead.Bead{
   219			ID:    "ddx-focus-active-worker",
   220			Title: "In-progress bead with live worker",
   221		}
   222		env := setupWorkFocusEnv(t, b)
   223	
   224		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(env.Dir, ddxroot.DirName))
   225		require.NoError(t, store.Claim(b.ID, "test-worker"))
   226	
   227		// Write a fresh worker status sidecar so the focus report can detect
   228		// the live worker without consulting the (stale-by-design) tracker
   229		// claim timestamp.
   230		workerID := "wkr-focus-active"
   231		require.NoError(t, workerstatus.WriteLiveness(env.Dir, workerID, workerstatus.LivenessRecord{
   232			WorkerID:       workerID,
   233			ProjectRoot:    env.Dir,
   234			CurrentBead:    b.ID,
   235			AttemptID:      "att-focus-001",
   236			Phase:          "running",
   237			Harness:        "claude",
   238			Model:          "opus",
   239			PID:            4242,
   240			LastActivityAt: time.Now().UTC(),
   241		}))
   242	
   243		root := NewCommandFactory(env.Dir).NewRootCommand()
   244		out, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "focus", "--json")
   245		require.NoError(t, err)
   246	
   247		var report WorkFocusReport
   248		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &report))
   249	
   250		require.Len(t, report.ActiveWorkers, 1,
   251			"focus must surface the live worker sidecar even when the tracker claim timestamp has not changed")
   252		assert.Equal(t, workerID, report.ActiveWorkers[0].WorkerID)
   253		assert.Equal(t, b.ID, report.ActiveWorkers[0].CurrentBead)
   254		assert.Equal(t, "att-focus-001", report.ActiveWorkers[0].AttemptID)
   255		assert.Equal(t, "running", report.ActiveWorkers[0].Phase)
   256	
   257		// The in_progress liveness "unknown" hazard must NOT be added when an
   258		// active sidecar is present — otherwise the focus report would
   259		// contradict itself.
   260		for _, u := range report.Unknowns {
   261			assert.NotContains(t, u, "worker process liveness",
   262				"active sidecar must suppress the in_progress liveness unknown")
   263		}
   264	}

package cmd

import (
	"context"
	"encoding/json"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"

	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/workerstatus"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func setupBeadHumanEnv(t *testing.T, beads ...*bead.Bead) (string, *CommandFactory, *bead.Store) {
	t.Helper()
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(workingDir, ddxroot.DirName))
	require.NoError(t, store.Init(context.Background()))
	for _, b := range beads {
		require.NoError(t, store.Create(context.Background(), b))
	}
	return workingDir, factory, store
}

func TestBeadNeedsHumanCommand_JSON(t *testing.T) {
	meta := bead.NeedsHumanMeta{
		Reason:          "review blocked",
		Since:           "2026-05-09T00:00:00Z",
		Source:          "pre-close review",
		SuggestedAction: "retry",
		Summary:         "review found missing tests",
	}
	nh := &bead.Bead{
		ID:       "ddx-needs-human-json",
		Title:    "Needs operator",
		Priority: 0,
		Status:   bead.StatusProposed,
		Labels:   []string{"area:cli"},
	}
	plain := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-plain-json", Title: "Plain"}
	_, factory, store := setupBeadHumanEnv(t, nh, plain)
	require.NoError(t, store.Update(context.Background(), nh.ID, func(b *bead.Bead) {
		bead.SetNeedsHumanMeta(b, meta)
	}))

	out, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "bead", "needs-human", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var rows []map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &rows))
	require.Len(t, rows, 1)
	assert.Equal(t, nh.ID, rows[0]["id"])
	assert.Equal(t, float64(0), rows[0]["priority"])
	assert.Equal(t, nh.Title, rows[0]["title"])
	assert.Equal(t, meta.Reason, rows[0]["reason"])
	assert.Equal(t, meta.Since, rows[0]["since"])
	assert.Equal(t, meta.Source, rows[0]["source"])
	assert.Equal(t, meta.SuggestedAction, rows[0]["suggested_action"])
	assert.Equal(t, meta.Summary, rows[0]["summary"])
	assert.NotContains(t, rows[0]["labels"], bead.LabelNeedsHuman)
}

func TestBeadStatusIncludesSixLifecycleStates(t *testing.T) {
	ready := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-status-ready", Title: "Ready"}
	dependencyRoot := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-status-dep-root", Title: "Dependency root"}
	dependencyWaiting := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-status-dep-wait", Title: "Dependency waiting"}
	dependencyWaiting.AddDep(dependencyRoot.ID, "blocks")
	inProgress := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-status-in-progress", Title: "In progress"}
	externalBlocked := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-status-blocked", Title: "Blocked"}
	proposed := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-status-proposed", Title: "Proposed", Status: bead.StatusProposed}
	closed := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-status-closed", Title: "Closed", Status: bead.StatusClosed}
	cancelled := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-status-cancelled", Title: "Cancelled", Status: bead.StatusCancelled}
	_, factory, store := setupBeadHumanEnv(t, ready, dependencyRoot, dependencyWaiting, inProgress, externalBlocked, proposed, closed, cancelled)
	require.NoError(t, store.Claim(inProgress.ID, "worker"))
	require.NoError(t, store.UpdateWithLifecycleStatus(externalBlocked.ID, bead.StatusBlocked, bead.LifecycleTransitionOptions{
		ExternalBlockerReason: "waiting on upstream release",
		Reason:                "test external blocker",
		Source:                "test",
	}, nil))

	text, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "bead", "status")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, text, "Open:")
	assert.Contains(t, text, "Lifecycle in progress:")
	assert.Contains(t, text, "Closed:")
	assert.Contains(t, text, "Blocked:")
	assert.Contains(t, text, "Proposed:")
	assert.Contains(t, text, "Cancelled:")
	assert.Contains(t, text, "Operator attention:")
	assert.Contains(t, text, "Worker ready:")
	assert.Contains(t, text, "Active workers:     1")

	out, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "bead", "status", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	var counts BeadStatusReport
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &counts))
	assert.Equal(t, 8, counts.Total)
	assert.Equal(t, 3, counts.Open)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, counts.InProgress)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, counts.Closed)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, counts.Blocked)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, counts.Proposed)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, counts.Cancelled)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, counts.NeedsHuman)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, counts.OperatorAttention)
	assert.Equal(t, 2, counts.Ready)
	assert.Equal(t, 2, counts.WorkerReady)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, counts.DependencyWaiting)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, counts.ExternalBlocked)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, counts.ActiveWork.Count)
	assert.Contains(t, counts.ActiveWork.BeadIDs, inProgress.ID)
}

func TestBeadStatusCountsFreshActiveWorkers(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir, factory, _ := setupBeadHumanEnv(t,
		&bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-status-active-a", Title: "Active A"},
		&bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-status-active-b", Title: "Active B"},
	)
	projectRoot := workingDir

	require.NoError(t, workerstatus.WriteLiveness(projectRoot, "worker-active-a", workerstatus.LivenessRecord{
		WorkerID:       "worker-active-a",
		ProjectRoot:    projectRoot,
		CurrentBead:    "ddx-status-active-a",
		AttemptID:      "att-active-a",
		Phase:          "running",
		LastActivityAt: time.Now().UTC(),
	}))
	require.NoError(t, workerstatus.WriteLiveness(projectRoot, "worker-active-b", workerstatus.LivenessRecord{
		WorkerID:       "worker-active-b",
		ProjectRoot:    projectRoot,
		CurrentBead:    "ddx-status-active-b",
		AttemptID:      "att-active-b",
		Phase:          "running",
		LastActivityAt: time.Now().UTC(),
	}))

	text, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "bead", "status")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, text, "Lifecycle in progress: 0")
	assert.Contains(t, text, "Active workers:     2")
	assert.Contains(t, text, "ddx-status-active-a")
	assert.Contains(t, text, "ddx-status-active-b")
}

func TestBeadStatusJSONReportsActiveWorkers(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir, factory, _ := setupBeadHumanEnv(t,

func TestBeadHumanResolveRetryRequiresNote(t *testing.T) {
	nh := &bead.Bead{
		ID:     "ddx-human-retry",
		Title:  "Retry human bead",
		Status: bead.StatusProposed,
	}
	_, factory, store := setupBeadHumanEnv(t, nh)
	require.NoError(t, store.Update(context.Background(), nh.ID, func(b *bead.Bead) {
		bead.SetNeedsHumanMeta(b, bead.NeedsHumanMeta{Reason: "blocked"})
	}))

	_, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "bead", "human", "resolve", nh.ID, "--action", "retry")
	require.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "--note is required")

	_, err = executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "bead", "human", "resolve", nh.ID, "--action", "retry", "--note", "operator approved retry")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	got, err := store.Get(context.Background(), nh.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, got.Status)
	assert.False(t, hasTestLabel(got.Labels, bead.LabelNeedsHuman))
	assert.Empty(t, bead.GetNeedsHumanMeta(*got).Reason)

	events, err := store.Events(nh.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NotEmpty(t, events)
	assert.Equal(t, "needs_human_resolved", events[len(events)-1].Kind)
	assert.Equal(t, "action=retry", events[len(events)-1].Summary)
	assert.Contains(t, events[len(events)-1].Body, "operator approved retry")
}

func TestBeadHumanResolveSplitObsoleteDefer(t *testing.T) {
	t.Run("split requires children and links them", func(t *testing.T) {
		parent := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-human-split", Title: "Split", Status: bead.StatusProposed}
		child := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-human-child", Title: "Child"}
		_, factory, store := setupBeadHumanEnv(t, parent, child)

		_, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "bead", "human", "resolve", parent.ID, "--action", "split", "--note", "split manually")
		require.Error(t, err)
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "--children is required")

		_, err = executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "bead", "human", "resolve", parent.ID, "--action", "split", "--children", child.ID, "--note", "split manually")
		require.NoError(t, err)

		got, err := store.Get(context.Background(), parent.ID)
		require.NoError(t, err)
		assert.False(t, hasTestLabel(got.Labels, bead.LabelNeedsHuman))
		assert.Contains(t, got.DepIDs(), child.ID)
	})

	t.Run("obsolete closes with evidence", func(t *testing.T) {
		nh := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-human-obsolete", Title: "Obsolete", Status: bead.StatusProposed}
		_, factory, store := setupBeadHumanEnv(t, nh)

		_, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "bead", "human", "resolve", nh.ID, "--action", "obsolete", "--note", "superseded")
		require.NoError(t, err)

		got, err := store.Get(context.Background(), nh.ID)
		require.NoError(t, err)
		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status)

package cmd

import (
	"context"
	"encoding/json"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"runtime"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/workerstatus"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestWorkStartup_ReapsStaleWorktrees(t *testing.T) {
	if runtime.GOOS == "windows" {
		t.Skip("process-liveness startup cleanup test is unix-oriented")
	}

	projectRoot, tempRoot := setupWorkStartupCleanupProject(t)
	t.Setenv("DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE", "1h")

	attemptID := "20260515T010203-deadbeef"
	worktreePath := createRegisteredStartupWorktree(t, projectRoot, tempRoot, "ddx-startup-stale", attemptID)
	old := time.Now().Add(-2 * time.Hour).UTC()
	writeStartupRunState(t, projectRoot, "ddx-startup-stale", attemptID, worktreePath, deadProcessPID(t), old)
	require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(worktreePath, old, old))

	root := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot).NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "--json", "--once", "--project", projectRoot)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var res struct {
		NoReadyWork bool `json:"no_ready_work"`
	}
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &res))
	assert.True(t, res.NoReadyWork)
	assert.NoFileExists(t, worktreePath)
	assert.NotContains(t, runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "worktree", "list", "--porcelain"), worktreePath)
}

func TestWorkStartup_PreservesLiveWorktrees(t *testing.T) {
	if runtime.GOOS == "windows" {
		t.Skip("process-liveness startup cleanup test is unix-oriented")
	}

	projectRoot, tempRoot := setupWorkStartupCleanupProject(t)
	t.Setenv("DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE", "1h")

	attemptID := "20260515T020304-feedface"
	worktreePath := createRegisteredStartupWorktree(t, projectRoot, tempRoot, "ddx-startup-live", attemptID)
	old := time.Now().Add(-2 * time.Hour).UTC()
	live := startLongLivedProcess(t)
	writeStartupRunState(t, projectRoot, "ddx-startup-live", attemptID, worktreePath, live.Process.Pid, old)
	require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(worktreePath, old, old))

	root := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot).NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "--json", "--once", "--project", projectRoot)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var res struct {
		NoReadyWork bool `json:"no_ready_work"`
	}
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &res))
	assert.True(t, res.NoReadyWork)
	assert.DirExists(t, worktreePath)
	assert.Contains(t, runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "worktree", "list", "--porcelain"), worktreePath)
}

func TestWorkStartup_ReapsStaleWorkerDirs(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot, _ := setupWorkStartupCleanupProject(t)

	workersDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName, "workers")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(workersDir, 0o755))

	deadWorkerID := "agent-loop-dead"
	staleWorkerID := "agent-loop-stale"
	freshWorkerID := "agent-loop-fresh"
	now := time.Now().UTC()

	require.NoError(t, workerstatus.WriteLiveness(projectRoot, deadWorkerID, workerstatus.LivenessRecord{
		WorkerID:       deadWorkerID,
		ProjectRoot:    projectRoot,
		PID:            deadProcessPID(t),
		LastActivityAt: now,
	}))
	require.NoError(t, workerstatus.WriteLiveness(projectRoot, staleWorkerID, workerstatus.LivenessRecord{
		WorkerID:       staleWorkerID,
		ProjectRoot:    projectRoot,
		LastActivityAt: now.Add(-31 * time.Minute),
	}))
	require.NoError(t, workerstatus.WriteLiveness(projectRoot, freshWorkerID, workerstatus.LivenessRecord{
		WorkerID:       freshWorkerID,
		ProjectRoot:    projectRoot,
		LastActivityAt: now,
	}))

	root := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot).NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "--json", "--once", "--project", projectRoot)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var res struct {
		NoReadyWork bool `json:"no_ready_work"`
	}
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &res))
	assert.True(t, res.NoReadyWork)
	assert.NoDirExists(t, filepath.Join(workersDir, deadWorkerID))
	assert.NoDirExists(t, filepath.Join(workersDir, staleWorkerID))
	assert.DirExists(t, filepath.Join(workersDir, freshWorkerID))
}

func TestExecutionsRetention_ArchivesOldEvidence(t *testing.T) {
	t.Run("archives old evidence under executions-archive", func(t *testing.T) {
		projectRoot := setupWorkStartupCleanupProjectRoot(t)
		t.Setenv(executionRetentionOverrideEnv, "7")

		oldTime := time.Now().AddDate(0, 0, -10).UTC()
		oldAttemptID := "20260101T000000-deadbeef"
		oldDir := setupWorkStartupEvidenceDir(t, projectRoot, oldAttemptID, oldTime)
		recentAttemptID := "20260501T000000-feedface"
		recentDir := setupWorkStartupEvidenceDir(t, projectRoot, recentAttemptID, time.Now().UTC())

		runner := newStartupHousekeepingRunner(projectRoot)
		report, err := runner.scan(context.Background(), true)
		require.NoError(t, err)

		archivedDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName, "executions-archive", "2026", "01", oldAttemptID)
		assert.NoDirExists(t, oldDir)
		assert.DirExists(t, archivedDir)
		assert.DirExists(t, recentDir)
		assert.Equal(t, int64(1), report.StaleExecutionDirs)
		assert.Equal(t, int64(1), report.ArchivedExecutionDirs)
		assert.Equal(t, int64(0), report.DeletedExecutionDirs)
	})

	t.Run("deletes old evidence when retain-days override is zero", func(t *testing.T) {
		projectRoot := setupWorkStartupCleanupProjectRoot(t)
		t.Setenv(executionRetentionOverrideEnv, "0")

		oldTime := time.Now().AddDate(0, 0, -10).UTC()
		oldAttemptID := "20260101T000000-cafebabe"
		oldDir := setupWorkStartupEvidenceDir(t, projectRoot, oldAttemptID, oldTime)

		runner := newStartupHousekeepingRunner(projectRoot)
		report, err := runner.scan(context.Background(), true)
		require.NoError(t, err)

		assert.NoDirExists(t, oldDir)
		assert.NoDirExists(t, filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName, "executions-archive", "2026", "01", oldAttemptID))
		assert.Equal(t, int64(1), report.StaleExecutionDirs)
		assert.Equal(t, int64(0), report.ArchivedExecutionDirs)
		assert.Equal(t, int64(1), report.DeletedExecutionDirs)
	})
}

func setupWorkStartupCleanupProject(t *testing.T) (string, string) {
	t.Helper()

	projectRoot, tempRoot := setupCleanupCommandProject(t)
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "init", "-b", "main")
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(projectRoot, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "add", "seed.txt")
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "commit", "-m", "chore: seed work startup cleanup repo")
	return projectRoot, tempRoot
}

func setupWorkStartupCleanupProjectRoot(t *testing.T) string {
	t.Helper()

	projectRoot := t.TempDir()
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName), 0o755))
	return projectRoot
}

func TestExecutionCleanup_DeletesOldWorkerDirs(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot := setupExecutionCleanupProjectRoot(t)
	tempRoot := t.TempDir()
	workersDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName, "workers")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(workersDir, 0o755))

	oldTime := time.Now().AddDate(0, 0, -20)
	oldWorker := filepath.Join(workersDir, "worker-old")
	recentWorker := filepath.Join(workersDir, "worker-recent")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(oldWorker, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(recentWorker, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(oldWorker, "status.json"), []byte(`{"id":"worker-old","state":"exited"}`), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(recentWorker, "status.json"), []byte(`{"id":"worker-recent","state":"running"}`), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(oldWorker, oldTime, oldTime))

	mgr := NewExecutionCleanupManager(projectRoot, &executionCleanupTestGitOps{})
	mgr.TempRoot = tempRoot
	mgr.RetainDays = 7

	summary, err := mgr.Cleanup(context.Background())
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.NoDirExists(t, oldWorker)
	assert.DirExists(t, recentWorker)
	assert.Equal(t, int64(1), summary.RemovedWorkerDirs)
	assert.True(t, hasObservationClass(summary.Observations, "removed_worker_dir"))
}

func TestExecutionCleanup_PreservesActiveItems(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot := setupExecutionCleanupProjectRoot(t)
	tempRoot := t.TempDir()
	oldTime := time.Now().AddDate(0, 0, -20)
	activeSessionID := "activesession123"

	// execution dir: active via run-state
	activeExecID := "20260101T000000-exec-active"
	activeExecDir := setupEvidenceDir(t, projectRoot, activeExecID, oldTime)
	require.NoError(t, WriteRunState(projectRoot, RunState{
		BeadID:    "ddx-active",
		AttemptID: activeExecID,
		SessionID: activeSessionID,
		StartedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
	}))

	// agent log: same session ID as active run-state
	logDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName, "agent-logs")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(logDir, 0o755))
	activeLog := filepath.Join(logDir, "agent-"+activeSessionID+".jsonl")
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(activeLog, []byte("data\n"), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(activeLog, oldTime, oldTime))

	// worker dir: PID 0 but not old enough to be pruned (use recent mtime to preserve)
	workersDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName, "workers")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(workersDir, 0o755))
	preservedWorker := filepath.Join(workersDir, "worker-preserved")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(preservedWorker, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(preservedWorker, "status.json"), []byte(`{"id":"worker-preserved","state":"running","pid":0}`), 0o644))
	// keep recent mtime so it is not pruned by age

	mgr := NewExecutionCleanupManager(projectRoot, &executionCleanupTestGitOps{})
	mgr.TempRoot = tempRoot
	mgr.RetainDays = 7

	summary, err := mgr.Cleanup(context.Background())
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.DirExists(t, activeExecDir)
	assert.FileExists(t, activeLog)
	assert.DirExists(t, preservedWorker)
	assert.Equal(t, int64(0), summary.RemovedEvidenceDirs)
	assert.Equal(t, int64(0), summary.RemovedAgentLogs)
	assert.Equal(t, int64(0), summary.RemovedWorkerDirs)
	assert.True(t, hasObservationClass(summary.Observations, "preserved_active_evidence_dir"))
	assert.True(t, hasObservationClass(summary.Observations, "preserved_active_agent_log"))
}

func TestExecutionCleanup_DefaultRetainDays90(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("HOME", t.TempDir())
	projectRoot := setupExecutionCleanupProjectRoot(t)
	configPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName, "config.yaml")
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(configPath, []byte("version: \"1.0\"\n"), 0o644))

	mgr := NewExecutionCleanupManager(projectRoot, &executionCleanupTestGitOps{})
	assert.Equal(t, 90, mgr.RetainDays)
}

func TestExecutionCleanup_RecordsCounts(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot := setupExecutionCleanupProjectRoot(t)
	tempRoot := t.TempDir()
	oldTime := time.Now().AddDate(0, 0, -20)

	// 2 old execution dirs
	for _, id := range []string{"20260101T000000-cnt-exec1", "20260101T000001-cnt-exec2"} {
		setupEvidenceDir(t, projectRoot, id, oldTime)
	}

	// 3 old agent logs
	logDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName, "agent-logs")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(logDir, 0o755))
	for _, name := range []string{"agent-s1.jsonl", "agent-s2.jsonl", "svc-s1.jsonl"} {
		p := filepath.Join(logDir, name)
		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(p, []byte("data\n"), 0o644))
		require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(p, oldTime, oldTime))
	}

	// 2 old worker dirs
	workersDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName, "workers")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(workersDir, 0o755))
	for _, id := range []string{"worker-cnt1", "worker-cnt2"} {
		d := filepath.Join(workersDir, id)
		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(d, 0o755))
		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(d, "status.json"), []byte(`{"id":"`+id+`","state":"exited"}`), 0o644))
		require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(d, oldTime, oldTime))
	}

	mgr := NewExecutionCleanupManager(projectRoot, &executionCleanupTestGitOps{})
	mgr.TempRoot = tempRoot
	mgr.RetainDays = 7

	summary, err := mgr.Cleanup(context.Background())
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Equal(t, int64(2), summary.RemovedEvidenceDirs)
	assert.Equal(t, int64(3), summary.RemovedAgentLogs)
	assert.Equal(t, int64(2), summary.RemovedWorkerDirs)
}

func hasObservationClass(observations []ExecutionCleanupObservation, class string) bool {
	for _, obs := range observations {
		if obs.Class == class {
			return true
		}
	}
	return false
}

func firstObservationMessage(observations []ExecutionCleanupObservation, class string) string {
	for _, obs := range observations {
		if obs.Class == class {
			return obs.Message
		}
	}
	return ""
}

package cmd

import (
	"encoding/json"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"

)

func newBeadTestRoot(t *testing.T, workingDir string) *CommandFactory {
	t.Helper()
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
	t.Setenv("DDX_BEAD_DIR", ".ddx")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxroot.InTree(workingDir), 0o755))
	return NewCommandFactory(workingDir)
}

func TestBeadCommandsCRUDLifecycle(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Fix auth bug", "--type", "bug", "--priority", "1", "--labels", "backend,urgent", "--acceptance", "bug is fixed")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	createdID := strings.TrimSpace(createOut)
	require.NotEmpty(t, createdID)
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(workingDir, ddxroot.DirName, "beads.jsonl"))

	showOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", createdID, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var created map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(showOut), &created))
	assert.Equal(t, createdID, created["id"])
	assert.Equal(t, "Fix auth bug", created["title"])
	assert.Equal(t, "bug", created["issue_type"])
	assert.Equal(t, "open", created["status"])
	assert.Equal(t, float64(1), created["priority"])

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "update", createdID, "--status", "in_progress", "--assignee", "me", "--labels", "backend")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	updatedOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", createdID, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var updated map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(updatedOut), &updated))
	assert.Equal(t, "in_progress", updated["status"])
	assert.Equal(t, "me", updated["owner"])
	require.Len(t, updated["labels"], 1)

func TestActiveWorkSnapshotIgnoresStaleWorkerSidecars(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot := t.TempDir()
	store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName))
	require.NoError(t, store.Init(context.Background()))

	staleSidecar := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-active-stale-sidecar", Title: "Stale sidecar"}
	staleClaim := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-active-stale-claim", Title: "Stale claim"}
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(context.Background(), staleSidecar))
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(context.Background(), staleClaim))
	require.NoError(t, store.Claim(staleClaim.ID, "worker-stale"))

	oldClaimTTL := bead.HeartbeatTTL
	oldLivenessTTL := workerstatus.LivenessTTL
	t.Cleanup(func() {
		bead.HeartbeatTTL = oldClaimTTL
		workerstatus.LivenessTTL = oldLivenessTTL
	})
	bead.HeartbeatTTL = -time.Nanosecond
	workerstatus.LivenessTTL = time.Second

	require.NoError(t, workerstatus.WriteLiveness(projectRoot, "worker-stale", workerstatus.LivenessRecord{
		WorkerID:       "worker-stale",
		ProjectRoot:    projectRoot,
		CurrentBead:    staleSidecar.ID,
		AttemptID:      "att-stale",
		Phase:          "running",
		LastActivityAt: time.Now().Add(-2 * time.Second).UTC(),
	}))

	snap, err := Collect(projectRoot, store, time.Now().UTC())
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Empty(t, snap.Records, "stale sidecars and stale claims must not count as active work")
	assert.Empty(t, snap.BeadIDs)
	assert.Zero(t, snap.Count)
}
